whatThisIs: Show toxic-threshold and therapeutic-range reference values for the drugs that show up in undifferentiated overdose and therapeutic-drug-monitoring workups: acetaminophen (Rumack-Matthew nomogram threshold at 4 hours), salicylates, digoxin (therapeutic 0.5-2.0 ng/mL, toxic >2.0), lithium (therapeutic 0.6-1.2 mEq/L, toxic >1.5, life-threatening >3.5), phenytoin (free vs total, albumin-corrected), valproate, carbamazepine, theophylline, methanol, ethylene glycol, iron, and the antibiotic peaks/troughs (vancomycin, aminoglycosides) that drive dose adjustment.
whenToUse: Use this in the emergency department when an overdose patient has a level back and the team needs the toxic-threshold bands for disposition (observation, antidote, hemodialysis), in the ICU when a therapeutic-drug-monitoring level is being interpreted for the next dose adjustment, or when teaching the levels above which a specific antidote (N-acetylcysteine, fomepizole, digoxin Fab, hemodialysis for lithium) is indicated. The numbers are reference - clinical context (time since ingestion, symptoms, renal function, comorbid ingestions) drives the decision.
